source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Hint: Please answer the question requiring an integer answer and provide the final value, e.g., 1, 2, 3, at the end.
Question: In the <image>, the logic gates shown in the digital circuit below use strong pull-down nMOS transistors for LOW logic level at the outputs. When the pull-downs are off, high-value resistors set the output logic levels to HIGH (i.e., the pull-ups are weak). Note that some nodes are intentionally shorted to implement "wired logic." Such shorted nodes will be HIGH only if the outputs of all the gates whose outputs are shorted are HIGH.
The number of distinct values of \( X_3X_2X_1X_0 \) (out of the 16 possible values) that give \( Y = 1 \) is ______.
extraction_notes: image field is a PIL image object placeholder, not a path/URL/filename

metadata.image_paths is empty while top-level image_paths contains 1 image path

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says "shown in the image," so the circuit topology is not fully recoverable from text alone
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the visual diagram is central
the task depends on interpreting gate connections, shorted nodes, and wired-logic behavior from the circuit image
multi_step: solving requires reading the circuit structure from the image and then reasoning about logic behavior under weak pull-up / strong pull-down assumptions
the prompt asks for the number of input assignments out of 16 that make Y=1, implying truth-table style enumeration or equivalent multi-condition simplification
wired outputs and shared nodes introduce combined conditions beyond a single direct lookup
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value ("8"), which is easy to compare against a computed result
the target is a finite count over 16 possible input combinations, so a verifier could in principle exhaustively check all cases once the circuit is parsed
asset_registry_record shows answer_present=true and registry_passed=true, supporting reliable downstream checking
